Seats per Screen / Auditorium Breakdown: 700 (single auditorium with main floor and stadium seating)
Auditorium Size Notes: Single-screen auditorium with backlit proscenium; stadium seating dates to 1949
Notable Amenities & Technology: Digital projection; Dolby Digital sound with sensurround subwoofers; high-backed rocker seats; real-butter popcorn
Programming Focus: Second-run and first-run films; cult classics; holiday movies; annual LOTR trilogy marathon; midnight movies
Demographics (Qualitative - Not Official: Broad neighborhood audience; families; budget-conscious moviegoers; nostalgia seekers; South Minneapolis locals
Sources / Notes: Designed by Liebenberg and Kaplan. Shifted to first-run films post-2020. Sources: Wikipedia; Riverview official site; Cinema Treasures

Auditorium Size Notes: 20-foot projection screen; expanded from 50 seats in 2017 renovation
Notable Amenities & Technology: Dual 35mm Century Model SA projectors; 16mm and digital projection; locally-sourced popcorn
Programming Focus: Strictly repertory: classic / arthouse / foreign / experimental / cult / B films
Demographics (Qualitative - Not Official: Dedicated cinephiles; film students; arthouse enthusiasts; all ages
Sources / Notes: MSR Design led 2017 pro bono renovation ($175K project). Operating budget covered by ticket sales and volunteers. Sources: Wikipedia; Trylon.org; Film Independent; Cinema Treasures

Address: 3951 Central Ave NE | Columbia Heights MN 55421
Auditorium Size Notes: Antique chandeliers with 2600 Egyptian lead crystals; 152 ceiling lights with hand-painted Edison Mazda bulbs
Notable Amenities & Technology: 1929 Wurlitzer pipe organ (4-manual / 16 ranks); 35mm reel-to-reel projection; 70mm projection; digital projection; motorized scarlet Grande drape
Programming Focus: Classic film series; first-run films; special events; premieres; pipe organ played Friday and Saturday evenings
Demographics (Qualitative - Not Official: Classic film fans; organ and music enthusiasts; older audiences; Columbia Heights and NE Minneapolis locals; film history buffs
Sources / Notes: Seat count has varied historically (500 original / 650 after 1934 reseating). Perry Crosier designed 1935 Art Deco interior remodel. Sources: Wikipedia; Heights Theatre official site; Cinema Treasures; YourClassical MPR

Seats per Screen / Auditorium Breakdown: 365 (single auditorium with stadium seats; couches; tray tables)
Auditorium Size Notes: Originally 630 seats; reduced to create stage/performance space
Notable Amenities & Technology: 35mm film projection; full bar; live performance stage; sound system upgraded 2007
Programming Focus: Hybrid venue: classic 35mm cinema; live music; comedy; podcasts; speaker series; first-run features
Ticket Price Range: $3-5 for movies; variable for live events
Demographics (Qualitative - Not Official: South Minneapolis locals; live-event attendees; cinephiles drawn to 35mm; 25-55 core demographic
Sources / Notes: Restored 2018 by owner Joe Minjares (Pepito's). Official rental specs cite 365; some sources cite 368 or 400 with 20 movable loveseats. Sources: Parkway official site; Cinema Treasures; Indie on the Move

Auditorium Size Notes: Two-story stacking of 5 auditoria (2 downstairs + 3 upstairs)
Notable Amenities & Technology: Wheelchair-accessible seating in each theater; elevator access; refurbished interior (2022)
Programming Focus: Hollywood blockbusters; international and foreign films; independent and arthouse films; classic films; hosts MSP International Film Festival
Ticket Price Range: $5 Tuesdays; standard pricing otherwise; student/child/young-adult discounts
Demographics (Qualitative - Not Official: Cinephiles; University of Minnesota students; international film community; downtown residents; festival-goers
Sources / Notes: Formerly St. Anthony Main Theatre. MSP Film Society took over operations in 2022; Film Society founded 1962. Historically had 70mm Norelco DP70 projection. Sources: MSP Film official site; Cinema Treasures; Yelp; Screendollars

Address: 760 Cleveland Ave S | St. Paul MN 55116
Seats per Screen / Auditorium Breakdown: Main auditorium ~550; second auditorium smaller (balcony conversion)
Auditorium Size Notes: Originally single-screen; balcony converted to second auditorium c. 1974
Notable Amenities & Technology: Luxury lounger seating (2018 renovation); upgraded concessions; preserved Art Deco marquee; lobby micro-museum
Programming Focus: Primarily first-run Hollywood films (NOT primarily classic films despite original framing); $5 Tuesdays with free popcorn
Demographics (Qualitative - Not Official: Highland Park neighborhood families; older St. Paul residents; multi-generational local regulars
Sources / Notes: Per-screen breakdown not publicly available. $1M renovation completed November 2018. Sources: Mann Theatres official site; Minnevangelist; Cinema Treasures

Auditorium Size Notes: 12 auditoriums with stadium seating and premium formats
Notable Amenities & Technology: Power-reclining leather seats; Dolby Atmos sound; 4K projection; EMX large-format screens; Coke Freestyle machines; full bar; stone-fired pizza; seat-side service; D-Box motion seats
Programming Focus: First-run Hollywood blockbusters; 'Retros at Willow Creek' classic/cult series; family films; horror; indie
Demographics (Qualitative - Not Official: West metro/Plymouth suburban families; couples seeking premium experience; nearby corporate workers (General Mills HQ nearby); broad demographic appeal
Sources / Notes: Emagine is privately held and does not publish per-location figures. Sources: Emagine official site; Atom Tickets; Yelp; Foursquare
